R2 domain-weighted convergence: Filament unanimous SHIP (0.6 default + render cue); MOTION DEFER (2/3 cool on pure rotation); DEFENSE SYNTHESIS -> ship CLEAN player-sever charge route only (neutralizes Grok's risk objections which targeted the multi-path absorb/Set/bundle, all cut) + armored render cue, Grok 'partial worse than none' LOGGED; G2-PROMO + G2-TRAITS DEFER (8x balance / §6 lock-amend). PRIME-AUDIT: A1 [CRITICAL] DEFENSE charge-bump rides INSIDE cause==='player' branch (physics/creature/bomb still bypass -> Diamond not indestructible, test physics-sever); A2 [HIGH] cues render-derived from synced combo type (world.effects host-local-only); A3 [MED] Filament double-count intended buff (doc it); A4 [MED] canSeverBond+computeBaseCharge same requiredCost (no negative charges); A5 [LOW] bespoke run-twice determinism tests.

priorities.label: S90 P1 — ECONOMY: Filament (Dot->Line) income trickle + render-derived earning cue
priorities.objective: A standing Filament bond earns FILAMENT_INCOME_COMPLEXITY=0.6 extra per-tick complexity on top of its magic-bond value (double-count INTENDED — Filament IS the income combo); visible via a render-derived 'income node' pulse computed client-side from the synced combo type.
priorities.scope: combos.ts isFilamentCombo helper; constants.ts FILAMENT_INCOME_COMPLEXITY=0.6 (doc intended double-count, #1 playtest knob); scoring.ts computeComplexity count filament bonds (fouled-skip inherited); axisAligned.ts drawFilament income-node ring + tests
priorities.testing: scoring.test.ts: held Filament adds 0.6 atop magic; fouled Filament earns 0; non-Filament magic unaffected; order-dependent (Line->Dot functional); run-twice byte-equal. session9 integration lock (real placement pipeline -> 4.6). bondVisualRenderer: income-node ring present + pulses on tick.
priorities.check_method: S90 P1 (a448fd6): Filament income trickle. isFilamentCombo(a,b) (resultName Filament, order-dependent Dot->Line). FILAMENT_INCOME_COMPLEXITY=0.6 counted INSIDE computeComplexity's bond loop (under the isMagical branch) so a Filament earns magic premium (+2.0) AND the +0.6 trickle — INTENDED double-count, doc-commented on the constant + computeComplexity JSDoc per PRIME-AUDIT A3 (an intended BUFF, NOT the S88-style bogus double-count to nerf). Inherits the fouled-skip (test: fouled Filament earns 0), single-owner attribution, mirrored scoreByPlayer sync; pure fn of synced state, host-only, NO wire/save change (AUDIT confirmed). CUE: drawFilament gains a pulsing 'income node' ring at the midpoint (circle+stroke, stroke-only per silhouette convention), render-derived from the synced combo type so BOTH peers see it (world.effects is host-local-only — AUDIT-2). RALPH:PATROL inline spec-conformance: matches PDR contract (extra-weight not parallel-add; order-dependent; fouled-skip; documented double-count; render-derived cue). UNIT: full vitest 1413/1413 (was 1407; +5 scoring [premium+trickle, order-dependence Line->Dot functional, non-Filament Cable no trickle, fouled=0, run-twice determinism] +1 bondVisual income-node; session9 'Dot->Line magic premium' test UPDATED to 4.6 + turned into an integration-level lock). tsc -b --noEmit exit 0. BUNDLE index 560.02 vite-kB = 546.9 KiB < 550 (+0.2 from S89 close). Full adversarial Triumvirate (Grok-ANALYST + Gemini-AUDITOR) deferred to the batch-end comprehensive audit (Rule 22) covering P1+P2 together. VISUAL GAP: the in-match income-node pulse needs a real Dot->Line built in a live game (Pixi not harness-drivable); render logic unit-proven (circle present + radius pulses on tick) + clean build.

Cue DEFERRED to v2 (existing distinct silhouettes give identity).

priorities.check_method: S90 P2 (f8adc57): DEFENSE Diamond/Lattice resist enemy sabotage. isDefensiveCombo(a,b) (resultName Diamond||Lattice, order-symmetric self-paired). DEFENSIVE_SEVER_CHARGE_COST=2 (==MAX_DISRUPTION_CHARGES). computeBaseCharge: hostile+defensive->2, else hostile->1, self/non-player->0. canSeverBond gates on `disruptionCharges < computeBaseCharge(...)` so the entry gate and the severBond.ts decrement AGREE BY CONSTRUCTION (PRIME-AUDIT A4). physics/creature/bomb bypass UNCHANGED (cause!=='player'->0) => a Diamond is NOT indestructible, resists only enemy player-RMB sabotage (PRIME-AUDIT A1 CRITICAL). Pure fns of synced state, host-only, NO wire/save/state. CUE DEFERRED to v2/G4 (existing distinct Diamond/Lattice silhouettes give identity; the 'it resisted' clang needs the host-local-effect visibility problem solved + playtest first). ADVERSARIAL CHECK (Triumvirate refute-first, covers P1+P2): GEMINI-AUDITOR PASS (Fidelity/Quality/Completeness 5/5/5 — faithful to contract, deferral documented). GROK-ANALYST raised 4 findings (1 CRIT/1 HIGH/1 MED/1 LOW); ALL FILTERED by inline Opus triage with concrete refutations: #1 'Filament order-dependence desync' REFUTED (bond aId/bId set host-authoritatively + SERIALIZED to clients => lookupCombo identical host/client/replay; the designed §V.1 order-dependence isMagical has used since S76); #2 'TOCTOU negative charges' REFUTED (synchronous single-threaded reducer; same primA/primB refs feed both calls with only pure severSplit between; placerColor+type readonly; gate calls the cost fn); #3 'fouled-skip desync' REFUTED (computeComplexity is HOST-ONLY, clients mirror scoreByPlayer, never recompute — S84 DISTRIBUTED-PIPELINE test); #6 'render leaks tick' REFUTED non-issue (keying animation on world.tick is the mandated convention drawWheel/Vortex/Orbital use, mutates no synced state, tick synced). Grok's attacks on the two REAL risks (no-indestructible softlock, new float-sum-order hazard) both FAILED => positive AUDIT-1 confirmation. CHECK verdict SHIP (2/3 PASS, Grok filtered). RALPH:PATROL inline: spec-conformant. UNIT: full vitest 1423/1423 (was 1413 after P1; +10 DEFENSE: cost-2 Diamond+Lattice, rejected-when-short, full-budget-breaks+left-at-0, physics/creature/bomb still sever a Diamond @0 charges [helper+orchestrator], self-sever free, non-defensive still 1). tsc 0. reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #the-cue-already-half-existed-ground-truth-the-renderer-first: the Council (Gemini, 1.75 on UX) was emphatic a silent Filament income bonus is 'a tree falling in an empty forest' — but reading the render layer FIRST showed each magic combo ALREADY draws a distinct persistent silhouette (drawFilament etc.) and the filament starburst ALREADY pulses on tick. So the 'render-derived, both-peers-visible' cue requirement (AUDIT-2) was largely MET by existing code; the gap was only 'show it's EARNING', not 'show it exists'. Added a minimal pulsing income-node ring rather than a speculative particle-flow-to-HUD (logged that as G4 juice, playtest-gated). LESSON: before building a 'we need a cue' feature, read the renderer — the visual identity may already exist; scope the addition to the actual gap, and don't gold-plate VFX before the mechanic is playtested.

P1 #a-special-combo-breaks-the-generic-combo-test: promoting Dot->Line's scoring (Filament trickle) broke exactly one existing test (session9 'a magic bond adds the magic premium') that had innocently used Dot->Line as a GENERIC magic example expecting complexity 4 — now 4.6. It went through the real placement pipeline, so rather than swap it to a non-Filament pair I updated it to 4.6 and re-purposed it as an INTEGRATION-level lock for the Filament behavior (proving the trickle survives placePrimitive, not just the unit-level addBond). LESSON: when you give one combo special behavior, grep for tests that used that specific pair as a stand-in for the generic case; a forced test update is often a chance to upgrade the test into a real regression lock.

P2 #derive-the-gate-from-the-cost-fn-so-they-cant-disagree: the DEFENSE premium needed BOTH the entry gate (canSeverBond) and the decrement (computeBaseCharge in severBond.ts) to know a Diamond costs 2. Rather than duplicate the isDefensiveCombo check in both (which invites the classic check-then-charge mismatch → negative charges), I made canSeverBond gate on `charges < computeBaseCharge(...)`. They now agree BY CONSTRUCTION — and the adversarial CHECK's TOCTOU finding was refuted precisely because the gate and the cost are the same pure fn over the same fetched-once refs in a synchronous reducer. LESSON: when a check and a mutation must use the same derived quantity, have the check CALL the quantity fn, don't re-derive it; single-source kills the TOCTOU class.

P2 #the-armor-cue-fought-three-geometry-tests-so-i-cut-it: I added a static 'armored' inner-plate to the Diamond/Lattice silhouettes for DEFENSE legibility (consistent with Filament's income-node), but it broke THREE precise geometry-lock tests (diamond closed-quad 1-moveTo/4-lineTo, diamond single-color 1-stroke, lattice 3-strokes). That churn was the signal: the addition fought the established visual contract, was speculative VFX I couldn't visually verify pre-playtest, and the real 'it resisted' moment needs a transient clang (blocked by world.effects being host-local-only) not a static plate. I reverted it cleanly (git diff vs the P1 commit confirmed byte-identical) and logged armored-glint + resist-clang as DEFENSE-v2/G4. LESSON: when a small render 'tell' breaks multiple exact-count geometry tests, treat the churn as a smell — rely on the existing distinct silhouette for identity and defer the juice to a playtest-gated pass; the Filament node was worth it (1 test added, 0 broken), the diamond armor was not (3 broken).

P2 #refute-first-CHECK-needs-supervisor-triage-against-the-execution-model: GROK-ANALYST (refute-first) returned a CRITICAL + a HIGH determinism/charge finding that LOOKED serious (order-dependent scoring desync; TOCTOU negative charges). All four were refuted by checking the ACTUAL execution model, not the abstract code: scoring is HOST-ONLY (clients mirror, never recompute), bond direction is SERIALIZED (so lookupCombo is identical everywhere), and the reducer is synchronous single-threaded with readonly placerColor/type (no interleaving, no mid-tick mutation). LESSON: an adversarial refute-first reviewer WILL produce plausible-but-wrong CRITICALs; the Supervisor must triage each against where-the-code-runs (host vs client vs replay) + the concurrency model + which fields are readonly, and FILTER with a concrete refutation — but the same pass also gave positive confirmation (Grok's attacks on the real risks, no-indestructible + float-order, both failed).

AUDIT #the-final-audit-earned-its-keep-on-the-test-NET-not-the-code: the ultracode 6-dim final audit confirmed the shipped code CLEAN (SHIP) but found a real MED gap one layer out — the byte-equal replay-determinism harness (save.replay.test.ts runStress) never called tickScoring and only ever did a cause:'physics' sever, so a Date.now/Math.random crept into the new S90 scoring path would have stayed GREEN (the auditor proved it with an injection). The bespoke run-twice scoring test I'd added was a near-tautology (pure fn over commutative integer counts) that gave false confidence. FIX: wire tickScoring into runStress so the scoring float-multiply lands in the compared snapshot. LESSON: a determinism GUARD only guards the reducer paths its stress script actually exercises — when you add a new per-tick reducer (scoring/physics force/charge), extend the shared replay harness to call it, or the guard silently doesn't cover the new code; and a 'deterministic' unit test over a pure integer fn is near-tautological — the real determinism net is the serialize-replay-compare harness.

AUDIT #ship-doesnt-mean-skip-the-doc-drift-sweep: verdict was SHIP, but the audit's anti-hallucination + Rule-22 dims surfaced that the BACKLOG roadmap still described 4 SHIPPED behaviors as forward work (incl. a false 'Vortex never built') and pinned scoring.ts:90 (P1 shifted it to :94). None touched runtime, but left unfixed they mislead the next session into re-building shipped work. LESSON: a passing audit verdict still warrants the doc-drift sweep — mark shipped behaviors shipped in the roadmap THIS session (strike+commit-tag), de-pin brittle file:line cites to symbol anchors, and keep SESSION HISTORY current; doc-drift compounds silently across sessions.
